question.text: In what scenario does appreciation of the local currency affect commodity futures prices?

answer.text: Futures prices may decline first, followed by spot price decline when currency actually appreciates
Futures prices increase as exports become more competitive
Futures and spot prices both increase

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency appreciation reduces INR prices of internationally traded commodities. Futures decline first in anticipation, and spot follows when appreciation materializes — contributing to backwardation. **The Trap:** Appreciation makes exports LESS competitive (not more). Both prices decline (not increase). Currency absolutely impacts futures. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I covers exchange rate impact on commodity pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When the rupee strengthens, commodity importers benefit from lower INR prices — this is why exporters may prefer to hedge currency risk separately.
